ac_ids_note: Per the orchestrator: no G8 AC exists. dev/acceptance.md is the LOCKED 0.6.0 file (max AC-073) with no G8/dangling/F10 entry; acceptance.md was NOT touched. Tests bind to gap G8/F10 + the pr_g8_dangling_edges.rs cases (a)-(f) (no case (g): strict mode deferred — see below).
findings_addressed: G8/F10 — additive, default-non-rejecting referential check at write time surfaced as WriteReceipt.dangling_edge_endpoints: u64 (Py+TS parity).
Cross-row correctness: same-batch later-inserted endpoint NOT flagged (the validate_write failure mode), proven by case (b).

tdd_evidence.red_commit_sha: fcdbd2b (post-rebase; pre-rebase b930c76) — RED via compile failure: 6x E0609 'no field dangling_edge_endpoints on WriteReceipt'
tdd_evidence.rust_cases: 7/7 pass: (a) one-missing→1, (b) same-batch-later-inserted NOT flagged→0, (c) edge→superseded node→2, (d) default flag-and-count commits, (e) sum-over-both-endpoints (both-missing→2), (f) EXPLAIN-QUERY-PLAN index-hit gate, + legacy NULL-logical_id baseline writes with defined count→2.

settled_probe_predicate.predicate: endpoint dangling when edge.superseded_at IS NULL AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM canonical_nodes WHERE logical_id=<endpoint> AND superseded_at IS NULL); from_id and to_id probed independently (0/1/2 per edge); missing OR superseded both count.
settled_probe_predicate.logical_id_alone: Probe is logical_id-ALONE, NOT (logical_id, kind): canonical_edges stores only the edge's own kind, not the endpoint node's kind, so the edges-carry-kind variant is ruled out by the landed schema.
settled_probe_predicate.index_cited: Hits the step-12 partial index canonical_nodes_logical_active_idx ON canonical_nodes(logical_id, kind) WHERE superseded_at IS NULL via its leading column + partial predicate. Prepared statement hoisted once outside the per-edge loop.
settled_probe_predicate.placement: Cross-row post-row-insert pass INSIDE commit_batch's open tx (after the batch loop, before enforce_provenance_retention/advance_projection_cursor/commit). commit_batch now returns rusqlite::Result<u64>; write_inner captures it and threads it into the receipt. NOT validate_write (single-row pre-insert, cannot see same-batch siblings). Edges iterated in-memory; an edge superseded within the same batch (later same-(Some(logical_id),kind) edge) is skipped to honor edge.superseded_at IS NULL.

additional_changes_made_in_scope: LEGACY-NULL ENDPOINT CONSEQUENCE (intended, informational, documented): only non-NULL logical_id nodes are valid endpoints. An edge pointing at a legacy/own-identity node (logical_id=NULL, byte-identical 0.7.x path) counts as dangling because NULL is not matchable by a logical_id probe. Correct per 'G8 rides on G0': the count is informational (flag-and-count, non-rejecting) and no current consumer sets logical_id, so NO existing behavior changes — only a signal is added. Documented in design memo + python/typescript-api.md.

STRICT MODE DEFERRED to reserved-gap BAND 22 (NOT built): there is no existing write-options/flag path on write_inner(&self, batch:&[PreparedWrite]) to thread, and inventing a public write-options surface is out of scope (scope discipline §6 / AC-057a-clean). Implemented FLAG-AND-COUNT default only. No test (g).

Native Python stub: added dangling_edge_endpoints:int to src/python/fathomdb/_fathomdb.pyi so pyright resolves the attribute access in engine.write/admin.configure (else 2 NEW pyright errors). This is the only non-obvious binding-surface addition.
Second WriteReceipt construction site (embedder-profile pin path, ~lib.rs:2624) set to dangling_edge_endpoints:0 — it commits no canonical edges.
Reverted an unrelated npm-install package-lock.json version-sync drift (0.6.1→0.7.2) to keep the change minimal/in-scope.

Anchor drift vs the prompt's pre-Slice-10/15 line numbers was minor; the re-verified anchors in the prompt's §3 (WriteReceipt :833, write_inner :1921/:1948/:1968, second receipt :2612, commit_batch :5727) were accurate at my baseline.
